paper_title: DNA Sequence Analysis and Character Based Identification

methods.description: This method involves assembling forward and reverse DNA sequences, aligning them, and manually adjusting them for further analysis.

steps.description: Following sequencing, the forward and reverse sequences were assembled using BioEdit ver. 5.0.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble forward and reverse DNA sequences using BioEdit

steps.description: Sequences were aligned using CLUSTAL X and manually adjusted in MEGA X.
steps.searchable_summary: align DNA sequences using CLUSTAL X and adjust in MEGA X

methods.description: This method uses BLAST algorithms to compare DNA sequences with GenBank sequences and applies a species threshold for delimitation.

steps.description: DNA sequences were compared with GenBank sequences using BLAST algorithms and a cut-off species threshold of 98.41% was used for delimitation of yeast species.
steps.searchable_summary: compare DNA sequences with GenBank using BLAST and apply species threshold

steps.description: The species that were first time barcoded were confirmed by double checking BLAST search similarity values and by searching for ITS gene sequences of the species in GenBank.
steps.searchable_summary: confirm new species barcoding by checking BLAST similarity and ITS gene sequences

methods.description: This method constructs a phylogenetic tree using the Neighbor-Joining method and evaluates it with bootstrap tests.

steps.description: Using the reference sequences extracted from GenBank, the Neighbor-Joining method was used for tree based yeast species identification.
steps.searchable_summary: construct phylogenetic tree using Neighbor-Joining method

steps.description: In the bootstrap test, the percentage of replicate trees in which the associated taxa clustered together (100 replicates) is indicated as circles next to the branches.
steps.searchable_summary: perform bootstrap test on phylogenetic tree with 100 replicates

steps.description: The NJ trees were manipulated in interactive Tree of Life (iTOL) database for better representation.

methods.description: This method describes the release of DNA sequences to GenBank and the Barcode of Life database for public access.

steps.description: DNA sequences generated in the present study were released to GenBank and could be accessed through accession numbers: KJ706221-KJ707237.
steps.searchable_summary: release DNA sequences to GenBank with accession numbers

steps.description: Entire dataset produced in this study could also be accessed through Barcode of life database under the project title 'DNA barcoding marine yeast' with a tag, 'DBMY' or through a digital object identifier.
steps.searchable_summary: make dataset accessible through Barcode of Life database and DOI

methods.method_name: Character Based Identification and BLAST Analysis
methods.description: This method involves character-based identification of ITS sequences and BLAST analysis to identify and confirm new yeast species.

steps.description: BLAST analysis revealed that 13 yeast species were barcoded for the first time and individual search of the ITS gene of those species in GenBank did not yield any results.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLAST analysis to identify new yeast species

steps.description: The overall intra-species K2P distance average was 0.33% which was well below the threshold proposed for the yeast species (1.59%).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate K2P distance for intra-species comparison
